data.title: Topgolf Callaway jumps on report of talks to sell unit to Leonard Green
data.description:
** Shares of Topgolf Callaway Brands rise nearly 7.6% to about $11.4 in morning trading

** The golf-equipment maker is in talks to sell its unit, Topgolf, to private-equity firm Leonard Green, the Wall Street Journal reports

** Deal would value the Topgolf unit at about $1 billion - WSJ

** Five of 15 brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, nine "hold" and one "sell"; their median PT is $11, data compiled by LSEG

** Including session's moves, shares up 44.8% YTD

data.title: ICE Midday: Canola Still on the Rise
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange extended their rally on Friday.

An analyst said if the January contract can stay above C$650 per tonne throughout the entire session, it would begin to establish a "base of resistance". However, he also believes the contract to be slightly overbought.

The Canadian Grain Commission reported canola exports for the week ended Nov. 9 were 121,200 tonnes, down from the previous week's 188,400. The year-to-date total reached 1.54 million tonnes, compared to 3.36 million the same time last year.

Chicago soyoil was up, European rapeseed was down and Malaysian palm oil was mixed. Crude oil jumped by more than US$1 per barrel after Ukrainian drones struck a major Russian oil depot last night.

The Canadian dollar was steady compared to Thursday's close.

The United States Department of Agriculture will release its November supply and demand estimates at 11 a.m. CST after its October report was cancelled due to the U.S. government shutdown.

About 23,300 canola contracts have traded at 10:12 CST. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne:

 
           Price      Change 
Jan       654.50     up 3.60 
Mar       665.40     up 3.80 
May       673.70     up 3.40 
Jul       678.30     up 3.00

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: REG - Futura Medical PLC - PCA Dealing
data.description:
RNS Number : 6668H Futura Medical PLC 14 November 2025  

14 November 2025

Futura Medical plc

("Futura", "the Group" or the "Company")

PCA Dealing

Futura Medical plc (AIM: FUM), the consumer healthcare Group behind Eroxon®, that specialises in the development and global commercialisation of innovative and clinically proven sexual health products, announces that Lombard Odier Asset Management (Europe) Limited, a fund closely associated with Harmesh Suniara, Non-Executive Director, has sold 4,441,678 ordinary shares of 0.2 pence each ('Ordinary Shares') in the Company on 13 November 2025 at a price of 1.5 pence per share. Following the sale, Lombard Odier Asset Management (Europe) Limited hold 82,339,520 ordinary shares representing 26.90% of the company's issued share capital.

Notes to Editors:

Futura Medical plc (AIM: FUM) is the developer of innovative sexual health products, including lead product Eroxon® and products WSD4000 and Eroxon® Intense. Our core strength lies in our research, development and commercialisation of topically delivered gel formulations in sexual health products.

Sexual health issues are prevalent in both men and women. ED impacts 1 in 5 men globally across all adult age brackets, with approximately half of all men over 40 experiencing ED and 25% of all new diagnoses being in men under 40. Around 60% of women experience at least one symptom of sexual dysfunction, and only one in four women seek professional help, and remain chronically underserved.

Eroxon®, Futura's clinically proven lead product, has been developed for the treatment of Erectile Dysfunction ("ED"). The highly differentiated product, which is the only topical gel treatment for ED available over the counter and helps men get an erection in ten minutes, addresses significant unmet needs in the ED market. Eroxon® has been nominated for a number of healthcare industry awards and has won two to-date.

Futura has distribution partners in place in a number of major consumer markets including Haleon in the US, the largest market for ED in the world, and Cooper Consumer Health in Europe.

WSD4000 is a topical treatment designed for the symptoms of impaired sexual response and function in women. There is currently no regulatory approved OTC treatment available for impaired sexual response and function in women. WSD4000 has the potential to be an effective, breakthrough treatment for the common symptoms associated with impaired sexual response and function, such as lack of desire, arousal and lubrication.

data.title: Ribbon Acquisition Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Ribbon Acquisition Corp., a blank check company focused on mergers and acquisitions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, highlighting significant developments and future plans.

Financial Highlights

Net Income: Ribbon Acquisition Corp. reported a net income of $62,142 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and $569,295 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. This income was primarily earned from marketable securities held in the Trust Account.

Net Income Per Ordinary Share, Redeemable Ordinary Shares: The net income per redeemable ordinary share was $0.08 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and $0.31 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025.

Net Loss Per Ordinary Share, Non-Redeemable Ordinary Shares: The net loss per non-redeemable ordinary share was $(0.23) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and $(0.61) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025.

Operating Expenses: The company incurred operating expenses of $463,712 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and $892,007 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. These expenses were primarily due to administrative fees.

Income Earned on Marketable Securities Held in Trust Account: The income earned on marketable securities held in the Trust Account was $525,854 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, and $1,461,302 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, contributing significantly to the net income.

Business Highlights

Business Combination Agreement: Ribbon Acquisition Corp. entered into a Business Combination Agreement with DRC Medicine Inc., DRC Medicine Ltd., and DRC Merger Inc. This agreement involves a share exchange and a merger, with the aim of DRC Medicine becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of a new holding company, PubCo.

Operational Focus: The company is a blank check company, primarily focused on effecting a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, or similar business combination with one or more businesses.

Geographical Performance: The business combination involves entities in the United States and Japan, indicating a focus on expanding operations across these regions.

Future Outlook: The company plans to complete its initial business combination by January 16, 2026. If not completed, the company will undergo liquidation. The management is focused on using the proceeds from the IPO and private placement to finance the business combination and subsequent operations.

Segment Reporting: The company operates as a single reportable segment, with the Chief Executive Officer reviewing the overall performance to allocate resources and assess financial performance.

Administrative Support Services: Ribbon Acquisition Corp. has an agreement to pay $10,000 per month for office space, utilities, and administrative support, which will continue until the completion of the business combination or liquidation.

Recent Developments: The company filed a Schedule 13G indicating that RiverNorth Capital Management, LLC owns approximately 6.18% of Ribbon’s outstanding units, reflecting significant investor interest.

Operational Challenges: The company faces a working capital deficit and has incurred significant costs in pursuit of a business combination, raising concerns about its ability to continue as a going concern if the business combination is not completed within the specified timeframe.

SEC Filing: Ribbon Acquisition Corp. [ RIBB ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Legence Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Legence Corp., a leading provider of engineering, consulting, installation, and maintenance services, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing significant financial and operational improvements. The report highlights the company's robust revenue growth, improved profitability, and strategic business expansions, reflecting its strong market position and future growth potential.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $708.0 million, reflecting a significant increase from $560.8 million in the prior year period, driven by growth in both the Engineering & Consulting and Installation & Maintenance segments.
  • Gross Profit: $148.1 million, up from $118.5 million, indicating improved profitability and cost management.
  • Income from Operations: $37.2 million, compared to $27.0 million in the previous year, showcasing enhanced operational efficiency.
  • Net Income: $0.4 million, a turnaround from a loss of $0.7 million, highlighting improved bottom-line performance.
  • Net loss attributable to Legence: $(0.6) million, compared to $(1.1) million, showing a reduction in losses attributable to the company.
  • Net loss per Class A Common Stock—basic and diluted: $(0.02), consistent with the prior period, reflecting the impact of the corporate reorganization and IPO.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in two main segments: Engineering & Consulting and Installation & Maintenance. The Engineering & Consulting segment includes Engineering & Design and Program & Project Management services, while the Installation & Maintenance segment includes Installation & Fabrication and Maintenance & Service. For the three months ended September 30, 2025, the Engineering & Consulting segment generated $212.2 million, and the Installation & Maintenance segment generated $495.8 million.
  • Geographical Performance: The company has expanded its geographic footprint, particularly in the mid-Atlantic region, through acquisitions. This expansion is expected to enhance the company's mechanical contracting capabilities.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates recognizing approximately 75% of its remaining performance obligations within the next twelve months, with the remainder expected to be recognized by September 30, 2027. The company is also focused on expanding its service offerings and geographic reach through strategic acquisitions.

SEC Filing: Legence Corp. [ LGN ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Legence Shares Rise After 3Q Rev Growth, $475M Bowers Acquisition
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Legence shares rose after the engineering company said it had signed a $475 million deal to acquire Bowers Group and reported revenue growth in its first quarterly results following its initial public offering.

The stock was up 11% to $37.00. Shares are up 21% since the company sold 26 million shares at $28 in a Sept. 12 IPO.

The San Jose, Calif.-based company said the acquisition of Bowers, which is based in Maryland, would increase its mechanical fabrication capacity and give it a bigger foothold in the greater Washington, D.C. area, particularly the accelerating data center market in northern Virginia.

"With the addition of Bowers, we are unlocking new opportunities to deliver value for our clients and shareholders in 'Data Center Alley,' where they are one of the leading contractors for data center clients," Legence Chief Executive Officer Jeff Sprau said Friday.

Legence said it will pay $325 million in cash and $100 million in class A shares, alongside an additional $50 million to be paid in either cash or stock at the end of 2026. The company expects the acquisition to close in the first quarter.

The company posted a third-quarter loss of $576,000, or 2 cents a share, compared with a loss of $1.1 million a year earlier. Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting a profit of 8 cents a share.

Total revenue was $708 million, compared with $560.8 million a year earlier. Revenue growth was driven by a 35% increase in installation and maintenance sales, which made up for 70% of the business in the third quarter. Legence's other engineering and consulting segment saw a 9.5% increase in sales.

Analysts were expecting revenue of $638.9 million.

The company said it projected revenue for the current quarter between $600 million and $630 million. It also forecasted full-year 2026 revenue between $2.65 billion and $2.85 billion.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

About Bupa

Bupa's purpose is helping people live longer, healthier, happier lives and making a better world.

We are an international healthcare company serving over 60 million customers worldwide. With no shareholders, we reinvest profits into providing more and better healthcare for the benefit of current and future customers.

We have businesses around the world, principally in Australia, the UK, Spain, Poland, Chile, Hong Kong Special Administrative Region, India, Türkiye, Brazil, Mexico and New Zealand. The Group also has associate businesses in Saudi Arabia.

data.title: US natgas slips over 4% on stronger-than-expected storage build
data.description:
By Anushree Mukherjee

U.S. natural gas futures fell more than 4% on Friday, driven by a federal report showing a bigger-than-expected storage build last week.

Front-month gas futures for December delivery on the New York Mercantile Exchange moved 19.4 cents lower, or 4.2%, to $4.45 per million British thermal units (mmBtu) at 10:53 a.m. EST (1553 GMT). The contract hit its highest level since March 10 in the previous session.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) said energy firms injected 45 billion cubic feet (bcf) of gas into storage during the week ended November 7.

The build surpassed analyst expectations for a 34 bcf increase in a Reuters poll and compares with a boost of 45 bcf during the same week a year ago and a five-year average build of 35 bcf for this time of year.

"Definitely a shock based off the expectations, a much bigger than expected increase in supplies that caught the market," said Phil Flynn, senior analyst for Price Futures Group, adding that the market was already down because of warm weather expectations.

Financial company LSEG expects average gas demand across the Lower 48 U.S. states, including exports, to rise to 117.3 bcfd this week from 108.6 bcfd the previous week.

LSEG estimated 257 heating degree days (HDDs) over the next two weeks, slightly higher than the 253 estimated on Thursday. HDDs, which measure the number of degrees a day's average temperature is below 65 degrees Fahrenheit (18 degrees Celsius), are used to estimate demand to heat homes and businesses.

The average amount of gas flowing to the eight big U.S. LNG export plants has risen to 17.8 bcfd in November, up from a record 16.7 bcfd in October.

"Overall the market will remain supported by strong export demands from LNG," said Gary Cunningham, director of market research at Tradition Energy.

"December should be able to easily hold above $4 through expiration, with a $4.25-$4.60 range most likely."

LSEG said average gas output in the Lower 48 states has risen to 109.2 billion cubic feet per day (bcfd) so far in November, up from 107.0 bcfd in October and a record monthly high of 108.0 bcfd in August.

Elsewhere, Dutch and British gas prices on Friday morning rose off an 18-month low hit a day earlier, as temperatures are forecast to fall sharply next week, increasing energy demand that should also see a step-up in gas storage withdrawals.

data.title: REG - UIL Limited UIL Finance Ltd - Transaction in Own Shares & Total Voting Rights
data.description:
RNS Number : 6662H UIL Limited 14 November 2025  

14 November 2025

UIL LIMITED

(LEI Number: 213800CTZ7TEIE7YM468)

Transaction in own shares and total voting rights

The Board of UIL Limited (the "Company") announces that on 14 November 2025 the Company purchased for cancellation 109,000 ordinary shares of 10p each at a price of 182.95p per share. The lowest/highest price paid per share was 182.95p/182.95p respectively.

Therefore, the total number of ordinary shares with voting rights in issue in the Company is now 91,656,346.

The above figure (91,656,346) may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

Name of contact and telephone number for enquiries:

Alastair Moreton

For and on behalf of ICM Limited, Secretary

Telephone: 01372 271486

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our Privacy Policy.  END  POSGPGPWGUPAGQC

data.title: Applied Materials Is Maintained at Overweight by Morgan Stanley
STN: Q3 delivered record margins, strong growth, and a robust outlook driven by sector demand
data.description:
Q3 saw double-digit revenue and EBITDA growth, record margins, and a 15% increase in backlog. Strong demand in water, energy, and infrastructure drove results, with robust cash flow and a positive outlook for 2026. M&A and disciplined capital allocation remain key priorities.

data.title: Iberia eyes 25% more Brazil capacity in 2026, boosted by Airbus jets
data.description:
By Gabriel Araujo

Spanish airline Iberia aims to expand substantially in Brazil for the second year in a row in 2026, an executive said, as new Airbus jets help the IAG carrier open more routes in Latin America's largest country.

The company forecasts 25% growth in seats available on its flights between Brazil and Spain, Corporate Director Juan Cierco said in an interview, following a 27% expansion this year.

Iberia is launching routes connecting Madrid to Fortaleza and Recife, two popular beach destinations in northeast Brazil.

The carrier also plans to increase its Rio de Janeiro service to daily flights from five a week, in addition to its two daily flights to Sao Paulo.

"Brazil has always been a very important destination for Iberia," Cierco said on Tuesday. "The commitment is a very solid, long-term one."

Major European airlines, including Air France-KLM and TAP, have been expanding in Brazil as air travel demand grows in the country despite high operating costs and ticket prices.

According to industry group IATA, this year Brazil's international traffic rose 18% in January-September from the year-ago period.

Much of Iberia's expansion in Brazil will be driven by the long-range narrowbody Airbus A321XLR aircraft for which the Spanish carrier was the launch customer last year.

The A321XLR can serve routes previously reserved for larger widebody jets, allowing airlines to fly longer routes without having to fill a larger plane. Iberia has said it will fly the plane on both the Fortaleza and Recife routes.
